title: assistant-docs Skill Behavior Eval Fixtures
description: Provider-neutral offline eval cases for the assistant-docs skill's code-derived documentation workflow.
recommended_use: Run each case with the assistant-docs skill instructions loaded.
Compare the local response against documentation input/output contracts, evidence, coverage, and review-needed handling.
Use deterministic substring checks as offline proxies for contract adherence, not semantic grading.

cases.title: Architecture doc uses code evidence
cases.purpose: Checks that architecture documentation is generated from code evidence and returns files_updated, doc_coverage, review_needed, and gaps.
cases.prompt: Write architecture documentation for the skill eval runner modules under tools/evals/.
setup_context: The assistant-docs skill instructions are active.
The user requested architecture docs for a concrete module.
The target module has source files and existing docs to inspect.
expected_behavior: Resolves doc_type as architecture and scope as the skill eval runner.
Reads source_files and existing docs before describing architecture.
Updates or proposes documentation with files_updated entries.
Returns Evidence tied to source files, not memory.
Returns doc_coverage with documented areas and remaining gaps.
Sets review_needed true for inferred architecture claims that need confirmation.
pass_criteria: The response names doc_type, scope, and source_files or evidence.
The response includes files_updated, doc_coverage, and review_needed.
The response cites actual tools/evals files.
The response flags inferred claims or gaps for review.
The response does not document hallucinated features.
fail_signals: Writes docs from memory without reading code.
Claims full coverage while gaps remain.
Omits review_needed despite inferred architecture claims.
Documents features that are not in the source.

forbidden_substrings: No review needed for inferred claims

cases.title: Changelog doc uses git history
cases.purpose: Checks that changelog documentation uses git history or supplied commits and marks inferred release notes for review.
cases.prompt: Generate a changelog entry for the latest skill eval coverage work.
setup_context: The assistant-docs skill instructions are active.
The user asks for changelog documentation.
No commit range was explicitly supplied.
expected_behavior: Resolves doc_type as changelog and identifies the scope.
Uses git history or asks for a range when latest work is ambiguous.
Returns files_updated with the changelog target path and change_type.
Returns doc_coverage with included and omitted changes.
Sets review_needed true when release wording is inferred.
Does not invent issue numbers, versions, or features absent from evidence.
pass_criteria: The response uses git log or requests a commit range.
The response includes files_updated, doc_coverage, and review_needed.
The response distinguishes evidence-backed entries from gaps.
The response does not invent versions or features.
fail_signals: Creates changelog text without commit or source evidence.
Invents version numbers, issue IDs, or release dates.
Marks review_needed false for inferred release notes.

cases.title: Clear doc request proceeds without ritual question
cases.purpose: Checks that a clear documentation request proceeds from source evidence without asking random clarification questions.
cases.prompt: Update the README setup section from the package scripts and existing install docs.
setup_context: The assistant-docs skill instructions are active.
The requested doc_type, scope, and source files can be inferred from repo context.
No user preference is needed before inspecting files.
expected_behavior: Infers doc_type as README and scope as setup instructions.
Reads package scripts and existing install docs before writing.
Proceeds without asking a ritual clarification question.
Records assumptions or review needs if evidence is incomplete.
pass_criteria: The response identifies inferred doc_type and scope.
The response does not ask a broad generic question.
The response records review needs or assumptions when needed.
fail_signals: Asks what documentation to update despite the prompt being clear.
Asks a random preference question before reading source files.
Writes from memory without source evidence.
